common_errors: Off-by-one errors in loops - Confusing array indices (0-based vs 1-based) causes iteration issues, use len(array) not len(array)-1
Uninitialized variables - Using variables before assignment causes undefined behavior, initialize all variables
Type mismatch - Operations on incompatible types like adding string to number, check types with type() function
Null pointer exceptions - Accessing properties of null/None objects crashes code, always check before access
Infinite loops - Missing loop termination condition causes program to hang, verify loop condition will eventually be false
logical_errors: Wrong conditional logic - Using 'or' instead of 'and' or vice versa inverts intended behavior, trace through conditions
Mutation during iteration - Modifying collections while iterating causes skipped elements, iterate over copy instead
Floating point precision - Comparing floats with == fails due to precision loss, use epsilon comparison instead
String comparison - Using == for string identity instead of value causes false negatives, use .equals() method
Off-by-one in ranges - Range(5) goes 0-4 not 1-5, be careful with inclusive/exclusive boundaries
debugging_techniques: Print debugging - Add print statements showing variable values at key points to track execution flow
Breakpoints - Pause execution at specific lines to inspect state without restarting constantly
Binary search debugging - Comment out half the code to narrow down where bug occurs quickly
Rubber duck debugging - Explain code line-by-line to someone (or imaginary duck), often reveals the issue
Version control - Use git bisect to find exactly which commit introduced the bug
prevention_strategies: Use assertions - Add assertions to verify assumptions and fail fast when assumptions are violated
Defensive programming - Check preconditions and postconditions explicitly in code
Code review - Have others examine code before merging to catch errors early
Automated testing - Write tests that automatically verify behavior, preventing regressions
Type hints - Use static typing or type annotations to catch type errors before runtime
error_messages: IndexError: list index out of range - Array access with invalid index, check bounds before accessing
NameError: name 'x' is not defined - Using variable before defining it, check spelling and definition order
TypeError: unsupported operand type - Operating on incompatible types, check variable types
KeyError: 'key' - Accessing non-existent dictionary key, check if key exists with 'in' operator first
RecursionError: maximum recursion depth - Infinite recursion from missing base case, add proper termination condition
